problem: The positive four-digit integers that use each of the four digits $1,$ $2,$ $3,$ and $4$ exactly once are ordered from least to greatest. What is the $15^{\text{th}}$ integer in the list?

classified_questions.question: What is the problem asking us to count?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many digits are we working with and how many different permutations can we make with these digits?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many permutations begin with the digit 1? How did we arrive at this number?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many permutations begin with the digit 2? How did we arrive at this number?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many permutations begin with the digit 3? How did we arrive at this number?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How could we continue finding all permutations until we get to the 15th integer in the ordered list?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How do we know that listing out the permutations in order from least to greatest will give us the 15th integer in the list?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Could we apply the same approach to finding the nth integer in a list of permutations using a different set of digits?
classified_questions.label: b
